hits.name: pyridoxal 5'-phosphate synthase (glutamine hydrolyzing)
hits.function: Catalyzes the formation of pyridoxal 5'-phosphate from ribose 5-phosphate (RBP), glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and ammonia. The ammonia is provided by PDX2. Can also use ribulose 5-phosphate and dihydroxyacetone phosphate as substrates, resulting from enzyme-catalyzed isomerization of RBP and G3P, respectively. Also plays an indirect role in resistance to singlet oxygen-generating photosensitizers

text:
The provided sequence corresponds to dipeptidyl-peptidase IV from the organism *Ascodesmis nigricans* [UniProt:A0A4V6RHE8].

### Protein Analysis
The protein is identified as **dipeptidyl-peptidase IV** [UniProt:A0A4V6RHE8]. It has a length of 813 amino acids [UniProt:A0A4V6RHE8].

**Function:**
It acts as a Type IV dipeptidyl-peptidase that sequentially removes N-terminal dipeptides from polypeptides [UniProt:A0A4V6RHE8]. This activity requires the polypeptides to have unsubstituted N-termini and specifically requires that the penultimate residue is proline [UniProt:A0A4V6RHE8].

### Evidence
* [UniProt:A0A4V6RHE8]
